**The O2 Arena**, locally referred to as **the O2**, is a multi-purpose indoor [arena](/wiki/Arena "Arena") in the centre of [The O2](/wiki/The_O2 "The O2") entertainment district on the [Greenwich Peninsula](/wiki/Greenwich_Peninsula "Greenwich Peninsula") in southeast London, England. It opened in its present form in 2007. It has the third-highest [seating capacity](/wiki/Seating_capacity "Seating capacity") of any indoor venue in the United Kingdom, behind [Co-op Live](/wiki/Co-op_Live "Co-op Live") and [Manchester Arena](/wiki/Manchester_Arena "Manchester Arena"), and in 2008 was the world's busiest music arena.[[1]](#cite_note-telegraph2008-1)

The arena and broader complex were built within the former [Millennium Dome](/wiki/Millennium_Dome "Millennium Dome"), a large dome-shaped building intended for an exhibition celebrating the turn of the third millennium. As of 2022, it is the [ninth-largest building in the world](/wiki/List_of_largest_buildings#Largest_usable_volume "List of largest buildings") by volume with a diameter of 365 metres (399 yards) and a height of 52 metres (57 yards). It is named after its primary sponsor, the telecommunications company [O2](/wiki/O2_(UK) "O2 (UK)"), a subsidiary of [Virgin Media O2](/wiki/Virgin_Media_O2 "Virgin Media O2"). The nearest tube station is [North Greenwich](/wiki/North_Greenwich_tube_station "North Greenwich tube station") on the [Jubilee line](/wiki/Jubilee_line "Jubilee line").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_O2_Arena&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Following the closure of the [Millennium Experience](/wiki/Millennium_Experience "Millennium Experience") at the end of 2000, the [Millennium Dome](/wiki/Millennium_Dome "Millennium Dome") was leased to Meridian Delta Ltd. in December 2001, for redevelopment as an entertainment complex. This included plans for an indoor arena.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Construction of the arena started in 2003, and finished in 2007. In December 2004, after the interior of the dome had been largely cleared and before building work inside began, the dome was used as the main venue for the annual Crisis Open Christmas organised by the London-based homelessness charity [Crisis](/wiki/Crisis_(charity) "Crisis (charity)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Owing to the impossibility of using cranes inside the dome structure, the arena's roof was constructed on the ground within the dome and then lifted; the arena building's structure was then built around the roof.[[2]](#cite_note-architect-2) The arena building, which houses the arena and the arena concourse, is independent from all other buildings in the O2 and houses all the arena's facilities. The arena building itself takes up 40% of the total dome structure.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The seating arrangement throughout the whole arena can be modified, similar to the [AO Arena](/wiki/AO_Arena "AO Arena") in Manchester.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The ground surface can also be changed between ice rink, basketball court, exhibition space, conference venue, private hire venue and concert venue.

The arena was designed to reduce echoing, a problem common among older arenas used for concerts.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Damage_to_The_O2_Arena%27s_tent_caused_by_Storm_Eunice.jpg/250px-Damage_to_The_O2_Arena%27s_tent_caused_by_Storm_Eunice.jpg)](/wiki/File:Damage_to_The_O2_Arena%27s_tent_caused_by_Storm_Eunice.jpg)

Damage to the O2's tent, above the roof of the O2 Arena, caused by [Storm Eunice](/wiki/Storm_Eunice "Storm Eunice") (2022)

On 18 February 2022 during [Storm Eunice](/wiki/Storm_Eunice "Storm Eunice"), large sections of the arena's fabric roof were shredded, causing the evacuation of 1,000 people, and the venue to close.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) It was later announced that repair works would take place and that the arena was likely to reopen on 25 February 2022, for a UB40 concert.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The repairs took place as planned, with the O2 Arena reopened as scheduled.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_O2_Arena&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Events")]

Main article: [List of events at the O2 Arena](/wiki/List_of_events_at_the_O2_Arena "List of events at the O2 Arena")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/63/The_O2_Arena%2C_Prince.jpg/250px-The_O2_Arena%2C_Prince.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_O2_Arena,_Prince.jpg)

[Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)")'s stage for his sold-out 21-night residency [The Earth Tour](/wiki/The_Earth_Tour:_21_Nights_in_London "The Earth Tour: 21 Nights in London"), 2007

The O2 Arena has hosted a variety of events, including but not limited to: sports events, concerts, music festivals, comedy shows, conferences, and the Olympics.

Musical artists such as [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)"), [Lady Gaga](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga"), [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), [Ariana Grande](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande"), [JoJo Siwa](/wiki/JoJo_Siwa "JoJo Siwa"), [Blondie](/wiki/Blondie_(band) "Blondie (band)"), [Little Mix](/wiki/Little_Mix "Little Mix"), [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna"), [Kylie Minogue](/wiki/Kylie_Minogue "Kylie Minogue"), [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"), [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson"), [Olivia Rodrigo](/wiki/Olivia_Rodrigo "Olivia Rodrigo"), [The Killers](/wiki/The_Killers "The Killers"), [Linkin Park](/wiki/Linkin_Park "Linkin Park"), [Shawn Mendes](/wiki/Shawn_Mendes "Shawn Mendes"), [Diljit Dosanjh](/wiki/Diljit_Dosanjh "Diljit Dosanjh"), [Gracie Abrams](/wiki/Gracie_Abrams "Gracie Abrams"), [Sabrina Carpenter](/wiki/Sabrina_Carpenter "Sabrina Carpenter"), [Tate McRae](/wiki/Tate_McRae "Tate McRae"), [Twenty One Pilots](/wiki/Twenty_One_Pilots "Twenty One Pilots"), [Katy Perry](/wiki/Katy_Perry "Katy Perry"), [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift"), [BLACKPINK](/wiki/BLACKPINK "BLACKPINK"), [MGK](/wiki/MGK "MGK"), [McFly](/wiki/McFly "McFly"), [Busted](/wiki/Busted_(band) "Busted (band)"), [Five](/wiki/Five_(group) "Five (group)"), [Def Leppard](/wiki/Def_Leppard "Def Leppard") and [Billie Eilish](/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish") have headlined concerts at the venue. Beginning in July 2009, American singer [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") was scheduled to hold a 50-show [residency](/wiki/Concert_residency "Concert residency") at the arena, titled [This Is It](/wiki/This_Is_It_(concert_residency) "This Is It (concert residency)").[[13]](#cite_note-Rolling_Stone_50_dates,_1_million_tickets-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) However, [he died](/wiki/Death_of_Michael_Jackson "Death of Michael Jackson") on 25 June, eighteen days before the first scheduled show.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

During the [2012 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2012_Summer_Olympics "2012 Summer Olympics") and [Paralympics](/wiki/2012_Summer_Paralympics "2012 Summer Paralympics"), the venue was referred as the **North Greenwich Arena** due to Olympics regulations regarding corporate sponsorship of event sites.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

The arena hosted the [ATP World Tour Finals](/wiki/ATP_World_Tour_Finals "ATP World Tour Finals") between 2009 and 2020.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/ATP_World_Tour_Final_Tennis_at_The_02_Arena_London.jpg/250px-ATP_World_Tour_Final_Tennis_at_The_02_Arena_London.jpg)](/wiki/File:ATP_World_Tour_Final_Tennis_at_The_02_Arena_London.jpg)

O2 Arena hosting a tennis match at the [ATP World Tour Finals](/wiki/ATP_World_Tour_Finals "ATP World Tour Finals")

Since March 2013, the arena has hosted [C2C: Country to Country](/wiki/C2C:_Country_to_Country "C2C: Country to Country"), Europe's largest [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") festival, which annually attracts over 20,000 fans. UK and Irish acts, as well as up-and-coming American acts, perform sets several times across various pop-up stages in and around the arena, with the main stage accessible only to ticket-holders. The seventh C2C was held on 8–10 March 2019.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

On 15 October 2022, the O2 hosted a boxing match between [Claressa Shields](/wiki/Claressa_Shields "Claressa Shields") and [Savannah Marshall](/wiki/Savannah_Marshall "Savannah Marshall") that was the first time two female boxers headlined at a major venue in the United Kingdom.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21) Shields won, and the fight headlined the first all-female boxing card in the United Kingdom.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

On 1 July 2023, the O2 Arena hosted [WWE Money In The Bank](/wiki/Money_in_the_Bank_(2023) "Money in the Bank (2023)"),[[23]](#cite_note-23) as well as the 30 June 2023 go-home episode of *[Friday Night SmackDown](/wiki/WWE_SmackDown "WWE SmackDown")* beforehand, which was the first time the show aired live on BT Sport (Now known as [TNT Sports](/wiki/TNT_Sports_(United_Kingdom) "TNT Sports (United Kingdom)")).[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On 2 November 2024, the O2 Arena hosted the finals of the [2024 League of Legends World Championship](/wiki/2024_League_of_Legends_World_Championship "2024 League of Legends World Championship").[[25]](#cite_note-25) [T1](/wiki/T1_(esports) "T1 (esports)") of the [League of Legends Champions Korea](/wiki/League_of_Legends_Champions_Korea "League of Legends Champions Korea") claimed their fifth title against [Bilibili Gaming](/wiki/Bilibili_Gaming "Bilibili Gaming") of China's [League of Legends Pro League](/wiki/League_of_Legends_Pro_League "League of Legends Pro League").

On 30 May 2025, heavy metal band [Babymetal](/wiki/Babymetal "Babymetal") became the first Japanese artist to headline, and sell out, a show at the O2 Arena.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

On 26 August 2024 at [All In](/wiki/All_In:_London_(2024) "All In: London (2024)"), [All Elite Wrestling](/wiki/All_Elite_Wrestling "All Elite Wrestling") (AEW) announced that the [2025 Forbidden Door](/wiki/Forbidden_Door_(2025) "Forbidden Door (2025)") will take place at The O2 Arena on 24 August 2025. The event broke the arena's record attendance for a professional wrestling event with 18,992 fans.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

On February 18th 2025, the O2 Arena hosted F1 75 Live, the first ever collective season launch in [Formula 1](/wiki/Formula_1 "Formula 1") history. Hosted by comedian [Jack Whitehall](/wiki/Jack_Whitehall "Jack Whitehall"), the event commemorated the sport's 75th anniversary year, featuring presentations from all 10 competing teams alongside musical performances by artists such as [MGK](/wiki/MGK "MGK"), [Kane Brown](/wiki/Kane_Brown "Kane Brown"), [Brian Tyler](/wiki/Brian_Tyler "Brian Tyler"), and [Take That](/wiki/Take_That "Take That").[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## Ticket sales records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_O2_Arena&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ticket sales records")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/Basket_Ball_at_the_O2_Arena.jpg/250px-Basket_Ball_at_the_O2_Arena.jpg)](/wiki/File:Basket_Ball_at_the_O2_Arena.jpg)

[New Jersey Nets](/wiki/New_Jersey_Nets "New Jersey Nets") v [Miami Heat](/wiki/Miami_Heat "Miami Heat"), an [NBA](/wiki/NBA "NBA") game in the O2 Arena

Despite being open for only 200 days per year (the equivalent of seven months), the venue sold over 1.2 million tickets in 2007, making it the third most popular venue in the world for concerts and family shows, narrowly behind the [Manchester Arena](/wiki/AO_Arena "AO Arena") (1.25 million) and [Madison Square Garden](/wiki/Madison_Square_Garden "Madison Square Garden") in New York City (1.23 million).

As of 2023, the O2 Arena was the third busiest music arena in the world in terms of ticket sales.[[29]](#cite_note-29)
